title: Headaches for Silicon Valley as China chips away at the US’s lead in the AI race
excerpt: Google’s AI struggles scream trouble as new Chinese models (again) throw US tech dominance into question
content_html:
Hello, I’m Blake Montgomery, writing to you after a double-header feature of Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey and the World Cup final. What a great Sunday. Today in tech, we’re discussing how China is chipping away at the US’s lead in the AI race and how Silicon Valley’s workers are taking action to protect their jobs from AI.

As Google falters on AI, China eats into the US’s lead

Google is struggling with the latest version of its Gemini model
A Chinese company, Moonshot, put out a free, surprisingly capable model last week
China’s new models yet again thrown the US dominance in AI into question

Google is months behind in its launch schedule for the new version of its flagship AI model, Gemini 3.5. According to Bloomberg, Google is attempting to improve Gemini’s coding abilities to catch up to Claude, the Anthropic bot that is widely considered the best AI coding tool. OpenAI released a new model at the start of July; Google had been expected to do the same in May. News of the delay sent Google’s shares sliding as investors questioned whether it could maintain its top position in the AI race.

A refreshed and newly capable Gemini might also have ameliorated the damage to the US’s reputation as the leader in AI this week – but didn’t.

As Google’s internal struggles became public, the Chinese startup Moonshot debuted an unexpectedly strong model, sending shivers through Silicon Valley and Washington DC alike. Moonshot’s Kimi K3 model featured surprisingly capable coding, particularly in a niche called “front-end coding”. “This may be the single biggest release of the year,” Anastasios Angelopoulos, the CEO of AI testing company Arena, said.

By Sunday night, Moonshot couldn’t keep up with the demand for its latest model. “Kimi K3 has received far more love than we expected, and our GPUs are feeling it. Over the past 48 hours, demand has pushed close to the limits of our current capacity,” the company posted on X.

Unlike ChatGPT, Claude and Gemini, Kimi K3 is free to use and open-weight, meaning anyone can download it and run it locally on their own computers, as its core components are released to the public.

It’s been the general strategy of Chinese AI companies: open-source and open-weight models, beating US AI companies on price as Chinese manufacturers do, and mirroring the broader trade war of the US and China. DeepSeek inspired a major US market panic last year. Alibaba previewed its latest model, Qwen3.8 Max, on Sunday, showing off a similar number of parameters as Kimi K3. The company plans to publish the model’s weights soon, per Bloomberg.

The strategy has been made possible by Beijing, which subsidizes computing resources and power consumption for its domestic AI startups, allowing them to release their AI models free of charge. The basic logic goes that if these startups can’t compete on capability with US models, they can compete on cost and undercut the US parent companies, which need to earn a profit eventually.

It’s all causing major headaches in Silicon Valley. There are no longtime incumbents in the AI business to whom customers will default. If those buyers, especially businesses who need lots of generated code, can turn to cheap or free alternatives, there might be little need to spend big on brand-name bots. The South China Morning Post, which is itself owned by the Alibaba Group, reported last week: “As US AI costs soar, global businesses pivot to China’s low-cost, open-weight models.”

In the US, nerves are rattling. Hawkish Trump administration officials are rekindling their efforts to block any US access to Chinese AI models via de facto bans, commerce department rules or an executive order, Axios reported on Monday.

OpenAI’s policy head for advanced AI, former Donald Trump adviser Dean Ball, wrote that Beijing’s open-weight approach to AI would lead to “full AI communism” with government-run datacenters powering AI as a public good. He denounced that future. OpenAI has been sounding the alarm about Chinese models for years, which reads as part political disagreement and part business strategy, since Chinese open-weight models threaten OpenAI’s projected profits.

“I would guess that the Trump Administration will at some point realize that their best strategy here would be to create large amounts of regulatory risk around the use of open-weight Chinese models,” Ball wrote.

There is no alignment between the US’s leading AI companies and the government players in the same industry. Emil Michael, the US Pentagon’s chief technology officer, responded to Ball: “Every industry/ecosystem has its supreme village idiot. Dean Ball is that for AI.”

As Beijing presents a unified front, the White House’s response is growing more scattered by the day. Trump himself had talked until recently of complete deregulation and never restraining any AI investment, research or releases. By contrast, the White House is restricting access – even allies’ access – to Anthropic and OpenAI’s advanced cybersecurity models. Even the personnel are changing. Chris Fall, the director of the Trump administration’s Center for AI Standards and Innovation, is resigning just three months into his tenure, Axios reported on Monday.

Not all of the AI crowd is against these models’ incursion on US dominance. Bill Gurley, a longtime venture capitalist best known for his early backing of Uber, wrote of China’s AI models in the Washington Post: “They aren’t a security threat – they’re what competition looks like.”

China’s advanced AI does pose a risk to the global business of AI, which is, as yet, still a race without a clear winner. Cheap Chinese AI models may trump their expensive US counterparts. The entire US economy is tied tight to the AI industry, raising the stakes of the Chinese models’ capabilities. Undercutting the US’s AI frontrunners’ business could unsettle the entire US stock market even more so than it already has.

That competition is set to play out in the future. In the present, though, China’s recent releases have thrown the politics of AI in the US into chaos. That reads as the bigger win.

Datacenters face global political blowback

  • New York becomes first state to impose one-year pause on new AI datacenters

  • Trump rails against New York’s statewide datacenter moratorium

  • Albanese’s AI blueprint sparks calls for datacentre moratorium until new regulations in place

Silicon Valley’s workers are worried about what AI is doing to their jobs

Employees are taking some of the US’s first collective actions to protect their jobs from AI

Last week, Meta workers filed a lawsuit against their former employer alleging that it automated their firing via algorithmic decision-making. Meanwhile, thousands of Google’s workers petitioned their CEO for layoff protections as the company shifts its spending from employees to AI infrastructure.

“Make no mistake: this is a company that is enjoying massive, unprecedented success,” Parul Koul, a Google software engineer and the president of the Alphabet Workers Union, said outside the company’s California headquarters. “These layoffs and cuts are not difficult decisions, but simply profit being put over the people that make this company run.”

These Silicon Valley employees are making these moves against the backdrop of the AI boom, which is changing software engineering rapidly and causing unions to gain popularity.

A Meta employee who was part of a recent union drive in the UK said: “It’s about us having a voice in all of the operations of the company, from products to employee experience.”

Meta countered last week’s lawsuit with an unusually direct statement: “These claims lack merit and are not based on facts. Workforce management and organizational decisions were and are made by people, not AI.” Google did not issue a statement in response to last week’s petition.

We’ve seen Silicon Valley luminaries warn of futuristic apocalyptic scenarios, but we’re seeing those worries come to life among the fearful rank-and-file employees, who are now taking action. We’re witnessing how those workforces, who have a front-row seat to AI’s development, respond to its effects on their employment.

Read more from Reworked, a Guardian series about what’s at stake as AI disrupts our jobs.

The wider TechScape

  • ‘Adversarial clothing’: are garments designed to confuse facial recognition systems about to go mainstream?

  • IBM loses quarter of its value as tech giant’s shares plunge and profits falter

  • ‘What’s the point?’ Teenagers give their verdict on Britain’s social media curfew

  • Amazon Web Services customers receive bills for up to $1.5tn after global glitch

  • Trump made $1.4bn from crypto in one year. Is Justin Sun the man who helped him do it?

sentences.zh: 多么美好的周日啊。

sentences.en: Today in tech, we’re discussing how China is chipping away at the US’s lead in the AI race and how Silicon Valley’s workers are taking action to protect their jobs from AI.
sentences.zh: 今天在科技领域，我们正在讨论中国如何削弱美国在人工智能竞赛中的领先地位，以及硅谷的工人如何采取行动保护他们的工作免受人工智能的影响。

sentences.zh: 他谴责了这种未来。

sentences.en: OpenAI has been sounding the alarm about Chinese models for years, which reads as part political disagreement and part business strategy, since Chinese open-weight models threaten OpenAI’s projected profits.
sentences.zh: 多年来，OpenAI 一直对中国模式发出警告，这部分被解读为政治分歧，部分是商业战略，因为中国的开放权重模型威胁到了 OpenAI 的预期利润。

blocks.html_en: “I would guess that the Trump Administration will at some point realize that their best strategy here would be to create large amounts of regulatory risk around the use of open-weight Chinese models,” Ball wrote.

sentences.en: “I would guess that the Trump Administration will at some point realize that their best strategy here would be to create large amounts of regulatory risk around the use of open-weight Chinese models,” Ball wrote.
sentences.zh: 鲍尔写道：“我猜特朗普政府在某个时候会意识到，他们最好的策略是围绕开放权重中国模型的使用创造大量监管风险。”

blocks.html_en: There is no alignment between the US’s leading AI companies and the government players in the same industry. Emil Michael, the US Pentagon’s chief technology officer, responded to Ball: “Every industry/ecosystem has its supreme village idiot. Dean Ball is that for AI.”

sentences.en: There is no alignment between the US’s leading AI companies and the government players in the same industry.
sentences.zh: 美国领先的人工智能公司与同一行业的政府参与者之间没有一致性。

sentences.en: Emil Michael, the US Pentagon’s chief technology officer, responded to Ball: “Every industry/ecosystem has its supreme village idiot.
sentences.zh: 美国五角大楼首席技术官埃米尔·迈克尔回应鲍尔：“每个行业/生态系统都有一个至高无上的村白痴。

sentences.en: Dean Ball is that for AI.”
sentences.zh: Dean Ball 就是人工智能的代表。”

blocks.html_en: As Beijing presents a unified front, the White House’s response is growing more scattered by the day. Trump himself had talked until recently of complete deregulation and never restraining any AI investment, research or releases. By contrast, the White House is restricting access – even allies’ access – to Anthropic and OpenAI’s advanced cybersecurity models. Even the personnel are changing. Chris Fall, the director of the Trump administration’s Center for AI Standards and Innovation, is resigning just three months into his tenure, Axios reported on Monday.

sentences.en: As Beijing presents a unified front, the White House’s response is growing more scattered by the day.
sentences.zh: 尽管北京呈现出统一战线，但白宫的反应却日益分散。

sentences.en: Trump himself had talked until recently of complete deregulation and never restraining any AI investment, research or releases.
sentences.zh: 特朗普本人直到最近才谈到完全放松管制，并且从不限制任何人工智能投资、研究或发布。

sentences.en: By contrast, the White House is restricting access – even allies’ access – to Anthropic and OpenAI’s advanced cybersecurity models.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，白宫正在限制（甚至是盟友的）对 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 先进网络安全模型的访问。

sentences.en: Even the personnel are changing.
sentences.zh: 就连人员也在发生变化。

sentences.en: Chris Fall, the director of the Trump administration’s Center for AI Standards and Innovation, is resigning just three months into his tenure, Axios reported on Monday.
sentences.zh: 据 Axios 周一报道，特朗普政府人工智能标准与创新中心主任克里斯·福尔 (Chris Fall) 在上任仅三个月后就辞职了。

blocks.html_en: Not all of the AI crowd is against these models’ incursion on US dominance. Bill Gurley, a longtime venture capitalist best known for his early backing of Uber, wrote of China’s AI models in the Washington Post: “They aren’t a security threat – they’re what competition looks like.”

sentences.en: Not all of the AI crowd is against these models’ incursion on US dominance.
sentences.zh: 并非所有人工智能人群都反对这些模型侵犯美国的主导地位。

sentences.en: Bill Gurley, a longtime venture capitalist best known for his early backing of Uber, wrote of China’s AI models in the Washington Post: “They aren’t a security threat – they’re what competition looks like.”
sentences.zh: 长期风险投资家比尔·格利（Bill Gurley）因早期支持 Uber 而闻名，他在《华盛顿邮报》上谈到中国的人工智能模型时写道：“它们不是安全威胁，它们就是竞争的样子。”

blocks.html_en: China’s advanced AI does pose a risk to the global business of AI, which is, as yet, still a race without a clear winner. Cheap Chinese AI models may trump their expensive US counterparts. The entire US economy is tied tight to the AI industry, raising the stakes of the Chinese models’ capabilities. Undercutting the US’s AI frontrunners’ business could unsettle the entire US stock market even more so than it already has.

sentences.en: China’s advanced AI does pose a risk to the global business of AI, which is, as yet, still a race without a clear winner.
sentences.zh: 中国先进的人工智能确实对全球人工智能业务构成了风险，迄今为止，这仍然是一场没有明显赢家的竞赛。

sentences.en: Cheap Chinese AI models may trump their expensive US counterparts.
sentences.zh: 廉价的中国人工智能模型可能会胜过昂贵的美国同行。

sentences.en: The entire US economy is tied tight to the AI industry, raising the stakes of the Chinese models’ capabilities.
sentences.zh: 整个美国经济都与人工智能产业紧密相连，这提高了中国模式能力的赌注。

sentences.en: Undercutting the US’s AI frontrunners’ business could unsettle the entire US stock market even more so than it already has.
sentences.zh: 削弱美国人工智能领跑者的业务可能会让整个美国股市更加动荡。

blocks.html_en: That competition is set to play out in the future. In the present, though, China’s recent releases have thrown the politics of AI in the US into chaos. That reads as the bigger win.

sentences.en: That competition is set to play out in the future.
sentences.zh: 这场竞争将在未来继续进行。

sentences.en: In the present, though, China’s recent releases have thrown the politics of AI in the US into chaos.
sentences.zh: 但目前，中国最近的发布已经让美国的人工智能政治陷入混乱。

sentences.en: That reads as the bigger win.
sentences.zh: 这看起来是更大的胜利。

blocks.html_en: Employees are taking some of the US’s first collective actions to protect their jobs from AI

sentences.en: Employees are taking some of the US’s first collective actions to protect their jobs from AI
sentences.zh: 员工们正在采取美国首批集体行动，以保护他们的工作免受人工智能的影响

blocks.html_en: Last week, Meta workers filed a lawsuit against their former employer alleging that it automated their firing via algorithmic decision-making. Meanwhile, thousands of Google’s workers petitioned their CEO for layoff protections as the company shifts its spending from employees to AI infrastructure.

sentences.en: Last week, Meta workers filed a lawsuit against their former employer alleging that it automated their firing via algorithmic decision-making.
sentences.zh: 上周，Meta 员工对他们的前雇主提起诉讼，指控其通过算法决策自动解雇员工。

sentences.en: Meanwhile, thousands of Google’s workers petitioned their CEO for layoff protections as the company shifts its spending from employees to AI infrastructure.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，随着公司将支出从员工转向人工智能基础设施，数千名谷歌员工向首席执行官请愿，要求获得裁员保护。

blocks.html_en: “Make no mistake: this is a company that is enjoying massive, unprecedented success,” Parul Koul, a Google software engineer and the president of the Alphabet Workers Union, said outside the company’s California headquarters. “These layoffs and cuts are not difficult decisions, but simply profit being put over the people that make this company run.”

sentences.en: “Make no mistake: this is a company that is enjoying massive, unprecedented success,” Parul Koul, a Google software engineer and the president of the Alphabet Workers Union, said outside the company’s California headquarters.
sentences.zh: “毫无疑问：这是一家正在享受巨大、前所未有的成功的公司，”谷歌软件工程师兼 Alphabet 工人工会主席帕鲁尔·库尔 (Parul Koul) 在该公司加州总部外表示。

sentences.en: “These layoffs and cuts are not difficult decisions, but simply profit being put over the people that make this company run.”
sentences.zh: “这些裁员和削减并不是困难的决定，而只是将利润放在让公司运营的人员身上。”

blocks.html_en: These Silicon Valley employees are making these moves against the backdrop of the AI boom, which is changing software engineering rapidly and causing unions to gain popularity.

sentences.en: These Silicon Valley employees are making these moves against the backdrop of the AI boom, which is changing software engineering rapidly and causing unions to gain popularity.
sentences.zh: 这些硅谷员工是在人工智能热潮的背景下采取这些举措的，人工智能热潮正在迅速改变软件工程，并使工会越来越受欢迎。

blocks.html_en: A Meta employee who was part of a recent union drive in the UK said: “It’s about us having a voice in all of the operations of the company, from products to employee experience.”

sentences.en: A Meta employee who was part of a recent union drive in the UK said: “It’s about us having a voice in all of the operations of the company, from products to employee experience.”
sentences.zh: 一位最近参加英国工会活动的 Meta 员工表示：“我们要在公司的所有运营中拥有发言权，从产品到员工体验。”

blocks.html_en: Meta countered last week’s lawsuit with an unusually direct statement: “These claims lack merit and are not based on facts. Workforce management and organizational decisions were and are made by people, not AI.” Google did not issue a statement in response to last week’s petition.

sentences.en: Meta countered last week’s lawsuit with an unusually direct statement: “These claims lack merit and are not based on facts.
sentences.zh: Meta 以异常直接的声明反驳了上周的诉讼：“这些说法缺乏依据，也没有事实依据。

sentences.en: Workforce management and organizational decisions were and are made by people, not AI.”
sentences.zh: 劳动力管理和组织决策过去和现在都是由人而不是人工智能做出的。”

sentences.en: Google did not issue a statement in response to last week’s petition.
sentences.zh: 谷歌没有针对上周的请愿书发表声明。

blocks.html_en: We’ve seen Silicon Valley luminaries warn of futuristic apocalyptic scenarios, but we’re seeing those worries come to life among the fearful rank-and-file employees, who are now taking action. We’re witnessing how those workforces, who have a front-row seat to AI’s development, respond to its effects on their employment.

sentences.en: We’ve seen Silicon Valley luminaries warn of futuristic apocalyptic scenarios, but we’re seeing those worries come to life among the fearful rank-and-file employees, who are now taking action.
sentences.zh: 我们已经看到硅谷名人对未来的世界末日场景发出警告，但我们看到这些担忧在恐惧的普通员工中变成现实，他们现在正在采取行动。

sentences.en: We’re witnessing how those workforces, who have a front-row seat to AI’s development, respond to its effects on their employment.
sentences.zh: 我们正在见证那些处于人工智能发展前沿的劳动力如何应对人工智能对其就业的影响。

sentences.en: Is Justin Sun the man who helped him do it?
sentences.zh: 孙宇晨是帮助他做到这一点的人吗？

content_html_zh:
大家好，我是布莱克·蒙哥马利，在看完克里斯托弗·诺兰的《奥德赛》和世界杯决赛的双头特写后给您写信。 多么美好的周日啊。 今天在科技领域，我们正在讨论中国如何削弱美国在人工智能竞赛中的领先地位，以及硅谷的工人如何采取行动保护他们的工作免受人工智能的影响。

随着谷歌在人工智能领域步履蹒跚，中国正在蚕食美国的领先地位

谷歌正在努力开发最新版本的 Gemini 模型 中国公司 Moonshot 上周推出了一款免费的、功能惊人的模型 中国的新模型再次让美国在人工智能领域的主导地位受到质疑

谷歌其旗舰人工智能模型新版本 Gemini 3.5 的发布计划比之前推迟了几个月。 据彭博社报道，谷歌正试图提高 Gemini 的编码能力，以赶上 Claude，后者是被广泛认为是最好的人工智能编码工具的 Anthropic 机器人。 OpenAI于7月初发布了新模型；谷歌预计在五月份也会采取同样的行动。 由于投资者质疑谷歌能否在人工智能竞赛中保持领先地位，延迟的消息导致谷歌股价下跌。

焕然一新、新能力的双子座本周可能也改善了美国作为人工智能领导者的声誉所受到的损害，但事实并非如此。

随着谷歌的内部斗争被公开，中国初创公司登月推出了一个出人意料的强大模式，让硅谷和华盛顿特区都感到不寒而栗。 Moonshot 的 Kimi K3 模型具有令人惊讶的编码能力，特别是在称为“前端编码”的领域。 人工智能测试公司 Arena 的首席执行官 Anastasios Angelopoulos 表示：“这可能是今年最大的一次发布。”

到周日晚上，Moonshot 已经无法满足对其最新型号的需求。 “Kimi K3 受到的喜爱远远超出了我们的预期，我们的 GPU 也感受到了这一点。 在过去 48 小时内，需求已接近我们当前产能的极限。”该公司在 X 上发布消息。

与 ChatGPT、Claude 和 Gemini 不同，Kimi K3 是免费使用且开放的，这意味着任何人都可以下载它并在自己的计算机上本地运行它，因为它的核心组件是向公众发布的。

这是中国人工智能公司的总体战略：开源和开放权重模型，像中国制造商一样在价格上击败美国人工智能公司，并反映了中美更广泛的贸易战。 DeepSeek 去年引发了美国市场的重大恐慌。 阿里巴巴周日预览了其最新型号Qwen3.8 Max，展示了与Kimi K3类似的参数数量。 据彭博社报道，该公司计划很快发布该模型的权重。

这一战略是由北京促成的，北京为国内人工智能初创公司提供计算资源和电力消耗补贴，允许它们免费发布人工智能模型。 基本逻辑是，如果这些初创公司无法在能力上与美国模式竞争，它们就可以在成本上竞争，并以低于美国母公司的价格竞争，而美国母公司最终需要赚取利润。

这一切都让硅谷非常头疼。 人工智能行业中不存在客户会默认的长期老牌企业。 如果这些买家，尤其是需要大量生成代码的企业，可以转向廉价或免费的替代品，那么可能就没有必要在品牌机器人上花大钱。 阿里巴巴集团旗下的《南华早报》上周报道称：“随着美国人工智能成本飙升，全球企业转向中国的低成本、开放式模型。”

在美国，人们的神经已经紧张起来。 据 Axios 周一报道，鹰派特朗普政府官员正在重新努力，通过事实上的禁令、商务部规则或行政命令来阻止美国获取中国人工智能模型。

OpenAI 高级人工智能政策负责人、前唐纳德·特朗普顾问迪恩·鲍尔 (Dean Ball) 写道，北京对人工智能的开放式态度将导致“全面的人工智能共产主义”，政府运营的数据中心将人工智能作为公共利益提供动力。 他谴责了这种未来。 多年来，OpenAI 一直对中国模式发出警告，这部分被解读为政治分歧，部分是商业战略，因为中国的开放权重模型威胁到了 OpenAI 的预期利润。

鲍尔写道：“我猜特朗普政府在某个时候会意识到，他们最好的策略是围绕开放权重中国模型的使用创造大量监管风险。”

美国领先的人工智能公司与同一行业的政府参与者之间没有一致性。 美国五角大楼首席技术官埃米尔·迈克尔回应鲍尔：“每个行业/生态系统都有一个至高无上的村白痴。 Dean Ball 就是人工智能的代表。”

尽管北京呈现出统一战线，但白宫的反应却日益分散。 特朗普本人直到最近才谈到完全放松管制，并且从不限制任何人工智能投资、研究或发布。 相比之下，白宫正在限制（甚至是盟友的）对 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 先进网络安全模型的访问。 就连人员也在发生变化。 据 Axios 周一报道，特朗普政府人工智能标准与创新中心主任克里斯·福尔 (Chris Fall) 在上任仅三个月后就辞职了。

并非所有人工智能人群都反对这些模型侵犯美国的主导地位。 长期风险投资家比尔·格利（Bill Gurley）因早期支持 Uber 而闻名，他在《华盛顿邮报》上谈到中国的人工智能模型时写道：“它们不是安全威胁，它们就是竞争的样子。”

中国先进的人工智能确实对全球人工智能业务构成了风险，迄今为止，这仍然是一场没有明显赢家的竞赛。 廉价的中国人工智能模型可能会胜过昂贵的美国同行。 整个美国经济都与人工智能产业紧密相连，这提高了中国模式能力的赌注。 削弱美国人工智能领跑者的业务可能会让整个美国股市更加动荡。

这场竞争将在未来继续进行。 但目前，中国最近的发布已经让美国的人工智能政治陷入混乱。 这看起来是更大的胜利。

数据中心面临全球政治反弹

  • 纽约成为第一个暂停新建人工智能数据中心一年的州
  • 特朗普批评纽约州范围内的数据中心暂停
  • Albanese 的人工智能蓝图引发了暂停数据中心直至新法规出台的呼声

硅谷的工人担心人工智能对他们的工作造成的影响

员工们正在采取美国首批集体行动，以保护他们的工作免受人工智能的影响

上周，Meta 员工对他们的前雇主提起诉讼，指控其通过算法决策自动解雇员工。 与此同时，随着公司将支出从员工转向人工智能基础设施，数千名谷歌员工向首席执行官请愿，要求获得裁员保护。

“毫无疑问：这是一家正在享受巨大、前所未有的成功的公司，”谷歌软件工程师兼 Alphabet 工人工会主席帕鲁尔·库尔 (Parul Koul) 在该公司加州总部外表示。 “这些裁员和削减并不是困难的决定，而只是将利润放在让公司运营的人员身上。”

这些硅谷员工是在人工智能热潮的背景下采取这些举措的，人工智能热潮正在迅速改变软件工程，并使工会越来越受欢迎。

一位最近参加英国工会活动的 Meta 员工表示：“我们要在公司的所有运营中拥有发言权，从产品到员工体验。”

Meta 以异常直接的声明反驳了上周的诉讼：“这些说法缺乏依据，也没有事实依据。 劳动力管理和组织决策过去和现在都是由人而不是人工智能做出的。” 谷歌没有针对上周的请愿书发表声明。

我们已经看到硅谷名人对未来的世界末日场景发出警告，但我们看到这些担忧在恐惧的普通员工中变成现实，他们现在正在采取行动。 我们正在见证那些处于人工智能发展前沿的劳动力如何应对人工智能对其就业的影响。

请阅读《卫报》系列节目《Reworked》中的更多内容，了解人工智能扰乱我们的工作所带来的利害关系。
